creator: enderx@uberhacker.org
text:
The current port of cdd will output ripped CD data only as a raw
.cda file, which can't be encoded as an mp3.

Fix: 

The patch at http://www.uberhacker.org/~enderx/cdd-1.0-patch.diff
adds a "-w" option to the program which will output audio tracks as
wav files for easy encoding. It has been tested successfully on
several differently configured systems (aka my friends all use it) ;-)
How-To-Repeat: "cdd -help" for command-line options, notice that none of them involve
wav files ;-)
